rows.llm_rationale: Current date is late April 2026, leaving ~7 months to year-end. Recent economic data (if available) would be critical, but absent real-time indicators, base rates suggest ~15-20% annual recession probability in normal conditions. The crowd price of 25.5% appears moderately hawkish on recession risk given the compressed timeframe and historical frequency, but reflects plausible tail risk from policy tightening or external shocks. Modest upward adjustment to 0.28 accounts for uncertainty in late-cycle positioning and policy risks not fully priced.
rows.question: US recession by end of 2026?

rows.llm_rationale: As of late April 2026, we're deep into the year with only 7 months remaining. If no cuts have occurred by now despite earlier expectations, the bar for cuts happening in the remaining period is high but not impossible. CME FedWatch data would show near-terminal rate expectations; the crowd at 47.4% YES appears slightly underpricing the probability of zero cuts given inflation persistence and Fed hawkishness typically required to skip an entire year. Modest upward adjustment to 52% reflects base rates of no-cut years (~40-50%) plus current late-cycle positioning.
rows.question: Will no Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?

rows.llm_rationale: As of late April 2026, we're 8 months into the year with limited time for rate cuts. Current Fed policy stance and CME FedWatch data typically show low probability for cuts in 2026 given recent inflation dynamics. The crowd price of 24.5% appears slightly underweighting tail risks of economic deterioration that could trigger emergency cuts, but the low probability reflects reasonable baseline expectations of continued elevated rates through 2026.
rows.question: Will 1 Fed rate cut happen in 2026?

rows.llm_rationale: As of late April 2026, the crowd price of 15.5% aligns with tight monetary policy expectations and recent Fed hawkishness. With only 8 months remaining in 2026, achieving 2 additional cuts from current levels would require significant economic deterioration or pivot. CME FedWatch data would show limited cut probability at this timestamp. The crowd price appears well-calibrated to fundamentals; slight upward adjustment to 16% accounts for tail-risk recession scenarios, but the base case supports the low probability.
rows.question: Will 2 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
